assertions.text: The vehicle was coming out of a curve immediately before sliding off the road.
The vehicle slid off the road (loss of traction or control resulting in leaving the roadway).
The vehicle struck (clipped) a utility pole, implying contact from the vehicle's side toward the pole.
The contact with the utility pole was light and glancing (minor impact).
The car required only cosmetic repairs after the collision (no major structural damage).
There was a single impact with the utility pole (one distinct contact).
No braking is mentioned; there is no claim that braking occurred before the vehicle slid off the road.

verdicts.evidence: The claim about coming out of a curve immediately before sliding is about road geometry/trajectory prior to leaving the roadway; telemetry shows steering activity with a peak of [22.5 deg](T2) at [t=-0.2 s](T2) and steering rate up to [48.0 deg/s](T2), but these channels do not document road curvature or confirm 'coming out of a curve'. The speed was [83.0 km/h](T2) at [t=-4.7 s](T2) and [80.0 km/h](T2) at [t=0.0 s](T2), which does not confirm the narrative detail.

Telemetry shows the vehicle speed was [80.0 km/h](T2) at impact (last pre-crash), and there is a large longitudinal crash pulse with final cumulative delta-V of [ -75.6 km/h](T2) indicating a major deceleration during impact; however, leaving the roadway (slid off the road) is a scene/trajectory fact not directly recorded by onboard sensors and is therefore unverifiable from the available channels.

The narrative asserts a side contact with a utility pole; the EDR records a dominant longitudinal cumulative delta-V of [ -75.6 km/h](T2) with a much smaller lateral cumulative delta-V peak of [ -8.8 km/h](T2) and final lateral of [ -6.9 km/h](T2). The device-frame axes and vehicle-frame alignment are undocumented, so inferring an impact direction as 'side toward pole' is not possible with certainty.

The claim of a light, glancing (minor) impact is contradicted by the recorded crash pulse: cumulative longitudinal delta-V reached [ -75.6 km/h](T2) at [t=0.24 s](T2), which indicates a high-severity impact inconsistent with a minor, light contact.

Whether the car required only cosmetic repairs is a post-crash damage assessment not measurable from telemetry. While the EDR shows a large cumulative delta-V of [ -75.6 km/h](T2) typical of significant damage, repair-level conclusions require visual/structural inspection and are therefore unverifiable from sensors alone.

The EDR summary and crash-pulse channels show a single dominant crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaching a single final value of [ -75.6 km/h](T2) and the delta-V arrays contain one main spike (no separate multiple impact pulses identified), supporting a single impact event.

The pre-crash brake channel shows brake was off for the whole pre-crash window with fraction_on [0.0](T2), first and last brake values [0](T2), and no transitions, supporting the statement that no braking was reported before the slide.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single high-severity impact (final cumulative longitudinal delta-V [ -75.6 km/h](T2)), no pre-impact braking ([0.0 fraction_on](T2)), and steering activity before impact ([22.5 deg peak](T2)). The severity contradicts the narrative claim of a light, glancing contact and cosmetic-only damage, making the overall narrative inconsistent with the sensor evidence.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
